creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
How NOT to start your London trip ❌

Getting from Gatwick Airport to Central London is fairly easy and contrary to popular belief, it doesn’t have to be super expensive 🤓

The Thameslink service can take as little as 29 minutes from Gatwick Airport to London Bridge and costs only £13.80 if you book in advance.

The Southern service to Victoria Station costs £14 (if you book in advance), and this train takes 32 minutes, which is only 3 minutes slower than the Gatwick Express (which costs £20.50). 

So there’s no need to take the Gatwick Express, but buying your ticket online before arriving at Gatwick Airport is a must, in my opinion 🤝

DM me “London” and get my free e-book with my top London travel advice 🤝  #london #gatwickairport #londontravel #traveltips

transcription.text: don't make this mistake when you fly into London Gatwick Airport. Even though Gatwick is located over 40k south of central London it's actually quite easy and fast to get into town. But there's one thing that always hurts my travel heart when I land in Gatwick and make my way through the airport. By the way hi there I'm Chris and I used to run a travel agency focused on London. Nowadays I'm sharing all my best travel advice on this account. So you can actually get from Gatwick to central London in just about 30 minutes by train. I would skip the dirty expensive Gatwick Express and just use the Southern or Thameslink trains. But no matter what train company you choose, make sure to buy your tickets online so you don't start your London trip by standing in this massive queue and miss your train. Just use an app like Trainline or Omeo, book your ticket and walk straight through the barriers and onto your train to London.
